content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5001 322-6441

June 15, 1976

Mr. Furman B. Roberts
City Attorney
300 East Chapman Avenue
Orange CA 92666

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 76-067

Dear Mr. Roberts:

Thank you for your letter of May 21, 1976, requesting an
opinion regarding City Councilman Jim Beam's acceptance of a
position with the Building Industry Association and the possible
ramifications. Since your letter raises no substantial ques-
tions involving an interpretation of the Act, no formal opinion
will be issued by the Commission pursuant to Government Code
Section 83114. However, I hope the following informal advice
of the Commission staff will be helpful.

The conclusion stated in your letter of May 21, 1976,
concerning Mr. Beam's right to hold office regardless of whether
he may have to register as a lobbyist is correct. However, as
Mr. Flores of our staff indicated to you on the telephone, local
public officials may not use their official positions to influence
a decision in which they know or have reason to know they have a
financial interest. Government Code Section 87100. In elabora-
tion of this prohibition, the Commission has adopted the follow-
ing regulation:

"In any way attempting to use his or her official position
to influence a governmental decision" shall include fur-
thering or attempting to affect in a manner any decision:

(1) Within or before his or her agency; or

(2) Before any agency which is appointed by or subject
to the budgetary control of his or her agency.

2 Cal. Adm. Code §18700(e)

As long as Mr. Beam does not appear before the Orange City Council
or any other agency over which the Council exercises budgetary
control, there will be no conflict within the meaning of 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18700(e).

Mr. Furman B. Roberts
Page Two

I believe Mr. Flores also pointed out that Mr. Beam must
disqualify himself from participation whenever any matter comes
before the City Council affecting the B.I.A. because, under
Section 87103 of the Government Code, the B.I.A. would be a
source of income to Mr. Beam and would be an entity in which
he holds a position of management.

I assume this letter and your telephone conversation
with Mr. Flores will resolve any concerns raised by Mr. Beam.
If, however, you wish to appeal the denial of your request for
a formal Commission opinion pursuant to Government Code Section
83114, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321.
If you have any further questions, please contact Mr. Flores
at (916) 322-6441.

Sincerely,

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:AF:pvp

JAMES BEAM
767 NORTH LINCOLN STREET
ORANGE, CALIFORNIA 92667
(714) 997-6664

May 10, 1976

Mr. Furman Roberts
Orange City Attorney
Civic Center
Orange, California

Dear Mr. Roberts:

Effective June 1, 1976 I will assume the full-time staff
position of Executive Director of the Orange County
Chapter of the Building Industry Association of Southern
California. The BIA of Southern California is the
largest regional trade association in the United States
and the Orange County Chapter is the largest chapter with
over 400 member companies.

As the executive officer of the Orange County Chapter it
will be my job to represent the construction industry to
the media, governmental bodies and the general public.
I will prepare and present industry position papers on
local, regional and national issues affecting the build-
ing industry such as land use, development ordinances
and codes, utilities, roads, energy availability, etc.
I will interface with other construction organizations,
labor, chambers of commerce, environmental groups and
other public interest organizations. I anticipate that
I will need to register as a legislative advocate with
the Orange County Board of Supervisors and perhaps some
local and regional governmental agencies, although
I have not yet verified this with the Legal Counsel for
BIA.

Would you please advise me in writing what impact, if any,
this new employment might have on my conduct as a City
Councilman? You will remember that in a telephone con-
versation of a few weeks ago, you responded to my
hypothetical question that you did not see any problem.
However, that was without knowing the specifics stated in
this letter and I would like a more formal reply.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
James Beam


city of orange

orange civic center • 300 east chapman avenue • orange, california 92666
post office box 449

city attorney
(714) 532-0391

incorporated 1888

May 21, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Request for Opinion


Gentlemen:

Enclosed is a letter from Mr. James Beam, City Councilman
of the City of Orange. You will notice that he intends to
assume the position of Executive Director of a Building
Industry Association. Even assuming that he is required to
register as a lobbyist, it seems clear to me from reading
Opinion No. 75-123 dated September 4, 1975, of 1 FPPC
Opinions 122 that there are no restrictions in the Political
Reform Act preventing a lobbyist from running for public
office. Certainly if the lobbyist is not prevented from
running for the office, it seems difficult to envision that
he would be prohibited from holding that office if he were
successful in his campaign.

I spoke to one of your attorneys in the Conflict of
Interest Section and he confirmed by understanding, however,
he suggested that I request a formal opinion. Therefore,
please consider this as a request for such an opinion.

As I understand the Political Reform Act, this act
only prohibits a lobbyist from conducting lobbying activities
in the public entity over which he exercises jurisdiction
as a public official. Mr. Beam assures me that the organization
would not conduct lobbying activities in the City of Orange.
If such ever occurred, naturally Mr. Beam would not participate
as a City Councilman in any way on any such issues in which
there might be a conflict of interest.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]

FURMAN B. ROBERTS
City Attorney

FBR:lrb

Enclosure

cc: James Beam

sections.question_synthetic: Can City Councilman James Beam accept a full-time position as Executive Director of the Building Industry Association while continuing to serve on the Orange City Council, and what restrictions apply to his conduct as a public official regarding this employment?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: City Councilman Beam may hold the position while serving on the Council and is not prohibited from registering as a lobbyist. However, he must not use his official position to influence any City Council decision or decision before agencies subject to Council's budgetary control that affect the BIA, and he must disqualify himself from any matter before the City Council affecting the BIA as it is a source of his income and an entity in which he holds a management position.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers. FPPC response is embedded within letter format. Confidence reduced due to informal advice designation and no formal conclusion section. Includes incoming request letter from City Attorney and councilman's initial inquiry letter, demonstrating full context of request.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can City Councilman James Beam accept a full-time position as Executive Director of the Building Industry Association while continuing to serve on the Orange City Council, and what restrictions apply to his conduct as a public official regarding this employment?

CONCLUSION: City Councilman Beam may hold the position while serving on the Council and is not prohibited from registering as a lobbyist. However, he must not use his official position to influence any City Council decision or decision before agencies subject to Council's budgetary control that affect the BIA, and he must disqualify himself from any matter before the City Council affecting the BIA as it is a source of his income and an entity in which he holds a management position.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue a formal opinion but provided informal staff advice regarding City Councilman James Beam's acceptance of an Executive Director position with the Building Industry Association, clarifying that while he may hold both positions, he must comply with conflict of interest prohibitions under Government Code Section 87100 and disqualify himself from matters affecting the BIA.
